free_text: Delayed neutron yields from fission of uranium-233,
Irradiations in a B4C filtered flux at the
Samples were encapsulated in stainless steel or
Neutron long counter. The neutron efficiency W
was measured by counting the delayed neutron emission
from U-235 for which the fission rate in the
irradiation position had been accurately determined.
Ge(Li) detector for monitoring activity of
fission products as a measure of the sample fission
Delayed neutron curves were fitted by five groups
with half-lives of 55,22,6,2,0.5 secs using a least
squares unfolding technique. A correction was made
for a sixth, unobserved, group with half-life 0.2 sec
Delayed neutron intensities and fission product
yields were corrected for impurities. As the transfer
time to counting position was 0.6 sec, delayed
neutrons with half-lives less than 0.5 sec were not
observed. A correction for a sixth group with a half-
life of 0.2 sec was made by empirical correlation
betwwen the yields and values of z and a of each
Errors are quoted as one standard
deviation, and are a combination of errors on neutron
intensities and those on fission rates in samples.
